title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This replayed kickoff episode from PwC’s Reporting reset series reviews foundational reporting and disclosure principles relevant for year-end reporting. It covers GAAP and SEC presentation requirements, differences between public and private financial statements, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent events, and includes links to PwC guidance and guest commentary from Pat Durbin and Diana Stoltzfus.
bullets: Re-release of the Reporting reset series kickoff focused on year-end reporting priorities.

Segmented topics with timestamps: GAAP & SEC requirements; reporting periods; balance sheet presentation; income statement structure; accounting changes/estimates/error corrections; subsequent events.
Provides direct links to PwC’s Financial statement presentation guide (Chapters 1, 2, 3, 28, 30).
Features Pat Durbin (PwC National Office Deputy Chief Accountant) with host Diana Stoltzfus (PwC partner, former SEC Deputy Chief Accountant).
Transcripts available on request; subscribe to the podcast/newsletter for updates.
takeaways: Ensure financial reporting practices align with foundational GAAP and any applicable SEC requirements for your entity type.
Review balance sheet classification and required disclosures to confirm significant assets and liabilities are presented appropriately.
Consider the choice of reporting periods and income statement structure when evaluating performance and communicating results.
Track accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events to assess and disclose their impacts on financial statements.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud provides a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions. It offers visibility to eliminate shadow AI, AI red teaming to stress-test models and applications, and an AI firewall that enforces real-time guardrails on prompts and responses.
bullets: AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across user interactions with AI systems.
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time prompt-and-response security with built-in guardrails.
A unified platform aimed at eliminating shadow AI and protecting infrastructure.
Available via DeepKeep on Google Cloud (visit https://www.deepkeep.ai/).
takeaways: Total visibility helps identify and eliminate shadow AI, reducing unmanaged AI usage risks.
Built-in red teaming enables proactive identification of vulnerabilities in models and apps.
Real-time AI firewall and guardrails help prevent unsafe or leaking prompts/responses.
A unified, cloud-based solution can centralize AI security controls for enterprise infrastructure.

items.title: On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing | Ryan Cochran-Siegle

ai.summary: Based on the available description: “Nothing is given, everything is earned.” For Ryan Cochran-Siegle, an Olympic legacy raises the standard rather than replacing the effort. Viewers are invited to follow Ryan and the U.S. Ski Team in ESPN Originals’ On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing at teamusa.com/tv and via Team USA’s social channels.
bullets: Tagline: “Nothing is given, everything is earned.”
Positions Olympic legacy as a motivator that increases standards and demands continued work.
Promotes ESPN Originals series On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing featuring Ryan Cochran-Siegle and the U.S. Ski Team.
Primary viewing destination: teamusa.com/tv.
Calls-to-action to subscribe/follow Team USA on YouTube, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok.
takeaways: Multi-platform promotion (site + YouTube + social) suggests planning for distributed traffic and scalable video delivery.
A centralized landing page (teamusa.com/tv) will likely be a focal point—prioritize uptime, load balancing, and analytics there.
Premieres of high-value sports content can create peak loads—ensure monitoring, auto-scaling, and CDN caching around release windows.
Track referral and engagement metrics from each social channel to inform capacity planning and optimize resource allocation.
Deliver low-latency, secure streaming and subscription flows to protect user experience and conversion during promotional pushes.

items.title: Lions, Koalas, & GPUs: Optimizing AI Inference

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses a zoo analogy to explain why treating all AI inference requests the same wastes GPU/TPU capacity and increases latency. It presents Google Cloud’s GKE Inference Gateway as a specialized routing and load‑balancing solution for AI/LLM workloads to improve utilization and performance.
bullets: Traditional load balancers treat every inference request identically, leading to wasted resources and slower responses.
GPU and TPU underutilization drives up costs for AI inference workloads.
GKE Inference Gateway provides inference-aware routing and load balancing tailored to specialized compute.
Focus is on optimizing LLM and real-time AI serving on Google Kubernetes Engine.
Video links to GKE Inference Gateway documentation for implementation details.
takeaways: Evaluate inference-aware load balancing to reduce GPU/TPU waste and improve latency for AI/LLM workloads.
Consider GKE Inference Gateway when running AI inference on Google Kubernetes Engine to better match requests to specialized compute.
Optimizing routing and utilization can help control cloud costs and improve real-time inference performance.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the promo frames Copilot as helping Christa "find the star within" against a sky full of stars. It closes with a call to action inviting viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Theme: Copilot helps Christa "find the star within"; imagery of a sky full of stars.
Clear call to action: Download & try Copilot via the link in bio.
Uses a star emoji to reinforce the message and tone.
Short, promotional description focused on user empowerment.
takeaways: Copilot is being promoted as a downloadable, consumer-facing tool — review distribution and access workflows for your environment.
Messaging centers on personal empowerment and ease of use — expect potential user adoption and related support needs.
The social CTA (link in bio) suggests public rollout channels — ensure security, authentication, and network policies are compatible before permitting enterprise use.
Track uptake and integration requirements if evaluating Copilot for employee or operational use.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this GeForce Garage episode announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest: u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The video lists the full parts and links, including chassis, GPU, motherboard, CPU, RAM, power supply, and AIO cooling.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard and Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU.
Memory and power: 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000 and Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU.
Cooling: Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO; full parts list linked via PCPartPicker.
takeaways: This build demonstrates a consumer example of packing an RTX 5090 into a small-form-factor chassis.
Component choices show a need for high-capacity SFX power (Corsair SF1000) when deploying top-tier GPUs in compact enclosures.
Use of a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) indicates attention to CPU cooling in tight SFF layouts.
Complete PCPartPicker list provides a practical parts reference for evaluating power, thermal, and space constraints for compact high-performance builds.
